REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, CROSS DEFENDANT LEW ROTHGEB, GREG BRYAN DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART. CROSS-COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES CAUSES OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF CC 1712 AND UNJUST ENRICHMENT AND DEMURRER TO THESE CASUES OF ACTION IS THEREFORE OVERRULED. DEMURRER TO THE CASUE OF ACTION FOR CONVERSION IS SUSTANINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS AS TO EACH ELEMENT OF THIS CAUSE OF ACTION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: J. MURPHY; REPORTED: PEGGY TSUJIMOTO CRS# 5229 (415)828-1919. (501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, CROSS DEFENDANT'S LEW ROTHGEB AND GREG BRYAN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE PLAIN MEANING OF SAN FRANCISCO RENT ORDINANCE 37.9A(E)(3) IS THAT ONE-HALF OF THE RELOCATION PAYMENTS "SHALL BE PAID AT THE TIME OF SERVICE OF THE NOTICE OF TERMINATION OF TENANCY." NOTHING ABOUT THIS LANGUAGE IS CONDITIONAL; THE ORDINANCE DOES NOT SAY, E.G., THAT THE AMOUNT IS MERELY TO BE "DEPOSITED" OR "LODGED." IT IS LOGICAL THAT A TENANT MAY WELL BEGIN THE EXPENSE OF SEEKING A NEW PLACE TO LIVE WHEN SHE RECEIVES SUCH A NOTICE. THUS, AS A MATTER OF LAW, CROSS-COMPLAINANTS CANNOT MAKE OUT A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONVERSION, AND THIS LEGAL ISSUE IS SUSCEPTIBLE TO DEMURRER. SEE, E.G., MOORE, 51 CAL.3D 120. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-06-2016 Continued To Aug-03-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-17-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Stephen Collier, Esq. (415-771-9850) for Plaintiff/Cross Defendant/Moving party LEW ROTHGEB and GREG BRYAN.
No other Appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT's LEW ROTHGEB and GREG BRYAN's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. The plain meaning of San Francisco Rent Ordinance 37.9A(e)(3) is that one-half of the relocation payments "shall be paid at the time of service of the Notice of Termination of Tenancy." Nothing about this language is conditional; the ordinance does not say, e.g., that the amount is merely to be "deposited" or "lodged." It is logical that a tenant may well begin the expense of seeking a new place to live when she receives such a notice. Thus, as a matter of law, cross-complainants cannot make out a cause of action for conversion, and this legal issue is susceptible to demurrer. See, e.g., Moore, 51 Cal.3d 120. Order is signed in open court. (see scanned order for details) Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
William Weisberg, (415)296-7070 for Cross-Complaintants/Moving parties Lew Rothgeb and Greg Bryan.
Stephen Collier, (415)771-9850 for Cross-Defendants/Opposing parties Greg DeForest et al.

Argued. The Court rules as follows: CROSS DEFENDANT LEW ROTHGEB, GREG BRYAN DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT OVERRULED in part and sustained in part. Cross-complaint sufficiently alleges causes of action for violation of CC 1712 and unjust enrichment and demurrer to these casues of action is therefore overruled. Demurrer to the casue of action for conversion is sustanined with leave to amend to allege facts as to each element of this cause of action. Order signed. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Reported: Peggy Tsujimoto CRS# 5229 (415)828-1919. (501)
